title: Canonical Event
description: A canonical event record — a temporally anchored historical occurrence (CIDOC CRM E5_Event). Phase 0 forward-declared; activation in P0.3 with the Salibiyyat layer (Crusader battles), Evliya Çelebi voyage events, and Bosworth dynasty foundings/falls. Events provide the connective tissue of historical narrative: they link persons, places, and dynasties at specific moments.

labels.description: Event name. Examples: prefLabel.en='Battle of Manzikert' / prefLabel.ar='معركة ملاذكرد' / prefLabel.tr='Malazgirt Savaşı'.

temporal.description: When the event occurred. REQUIRED for events. Day-precision when known (iso_start_date); otherwise year-precision.

location.description: Primary location of the event. PID of iac:place-.

secondary_locations.description: Additional locations involved (e.g., for a multi-day battle that spread across multiple sites, or a treaty signed in one place but ratified in another).

participants_dynasties.description: Dynasties involved (sides in a battle, parties to a treaty, founder of a city).

outcome.description: Coarse outcome label, when applicable. Detailed narrative goes in note.

victor_dynasty.description: If outcome is victory-tagged, the victor dynasty PID.

preceded_by.description: Events that immediately preceded this one (causally or chronologically).

causes.description: Events asserted as causes (stronger semantic than preceded_by).

consequences.description: Events asserted as consequences (stronger semantic than followed_by).

casualties.description: Casualty figures (when reported by sources). Often varies wildly across pre-modern sources; record the most authoritative scholarly estimate and cite via source_estimate.

note.description: Free-text editorial narrative — events warrant longer notes than other entity types because the chronological detail is often the most interesting research substrate.

$comment: Phase 0.3 hard rules: (a) at least one of location / secondary_locations required; (b) at least one of participants_persons / participants_dynasties required (an event without participants is a non-event); (c) preceded_by / followed_by bidirectional consistency enforced post-canonicalization.
